mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, which increases present equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency during FEDFUNDS declines is not significantly above 50% (binomial p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate above 6.0 percent signals accelerating labor market deterioration that erodes consumer spending and corporate profit expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day negative return frequency when UNRATE > 6.0% is below 55% over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal's Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls indicate a coordinated collapse in aggregate demand and labor income that is stronger than either signal in isolation, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not produce significantly more negative 60-day returns than single-series declines (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production predict negative S&P 500 returns during periods of elevated unemployment above 5.5 percent because manufacturing contraction coincides with weak labor demand, amplifying recession risk.
regime_hint: elevated unemployment regime with UNRATE above 5.5 percent
failure_condition: Declines in INDPRO fail to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when UNRATE > 5.5% during any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 900 thousand units signal a deep contraction in residential construction and household wealth destruction that spills into equity markets within days, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST below 900 fails to coincide with negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 2.0 percent reflects aggressive monetary easing that lowers borrowing costs and discount rates sufficiently to dominate near-term credit risk concerns, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS below 2.0% fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in retail sales signal weakening consumer demand and prompt downward revisions to corporate revenue expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS declines fail to coincide with negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in the unemployment rate and the consumer price index create a stagflationary environment that compresses real household incomes and corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint increases in UNRATE and CPIAUCSL fail to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of joint-increase months over any 3-year rolling window.
